steps.action: Marinera is a partner dance that originated alon with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat.
steps.text: Marinera is a partner dance that originated alon with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat.

steps.action: The dance is a re-enactment of an ancient Mochic
steps.text: The dance is a re-enactment of an ancient Mochic dance, modernised with a mix of Spanish contradanza and Andean zamacueca, and is a stylized reenactment of a courtship, showing a blend of the different cultures of Peru.

steps.action: The dance has gained recognition throughout Sout
steps.text: The dance has gained recognition throughout South America and is known as the most prominent traditional dance of Peru.

steps.action: These annual competitions of the dance have take
steps.text: These annual competitions of the dance have taken place since 1960.

steps.action: The dance is traditionally accompanied by severa
steps.text: The dance is traditionally accompanied by several instruments: cajón, clarinets, guitars, drums, and bugles.

steps.action: Although the dance had informally been around in
steps.text: Although the dance had informally been around in Peru since the colonial era, it was formally recognized as "Marinera" in honor of the Navy of Peru, or the Marina de Guerra del Peru in 1879 during the War of the Pacific.

steps.action: According to the Peruvian historian Rómulo Cúneo
steps.text: According to the Peruvian historian Rómulo Cúneo y Vidal, the Zamacueca was itself a dance of rest during the era of the Inca Empire.

steps.action: Thus, coming from such a far natively Peruvian b
steps.text: Thus, coming from such a far natively Peruvian background, the dance is itself simply a derivation of an ancient Peruvian dance.

steps.action: There are Marinera dance academies all over Peru
steps.text: There are Marinera dance academies all over Peru, and competitions are frequently held.

steps.action: Sometimes the Marinera is danced with her partne
steps.text: Sometimes the Marinera is danced with her partner mounted on a Peruvian Paso (the horse dancing, and the chálan directing it).

steps.action: The dance can be interpreted in low or high tone
steps.text: The dance can be interpreted in low or high tones.

steps.action: Nevertheless, the dance still has a small number
steps.text: Nevertheless, the dance still has a small number of fans that dance it during October fests or during the anniversary of Lima.

steps.action: The dance itself tends to be quick-paced and tho
steps.text: The dance itself tends to be quick-paced and though not as "elegant" as the Limeña, it can also be very stylish.

steps.action: Even though the dance originated in the Northern
steps.text: Even though the dance originated in the Northern coasts of the country, it has become quite popular throughout Peru.

steps.action: It is thanks to that popularity that the Mariner
steps.text: It is thanks to that popularity that the Marinera is considered the National Dance of Peru, along with the Peruvian Waltz.

steps.action: In Marinera Norteña, the man wears shoes, while
steps.text: In Marinera Norteña, the man wears shoes, while the woman dances completely barefoot.

steps.action: The dancer must go to the dance floor wearing th
steps.text: The dancer must go to the dance floor wearing their best clothes but with bare feet, in the same way they did the rural northern girls of the nineteenth century.

steps.action: Being forced to dance barefoot on any surface wi
steps.text: Being forced to dance barefoot on any surface without showing any discomfort, professional dancers should practice enough to develop thick calluses on the soles of their feet.

steps.action: " "The Marinera Norteña allows men to wear shoes
steps.text: " "The Marinera Norteña allows men to wear shoes, but women must dance barefoot, soon developing thick calluses on their feet, of which they are proud to show off.

steps.action: " "It was in Trujillo that female dancers starte
steps.text: " "It was in Trujillo that female dancers started the whole tradition of going barefoot—some even pride themselves on being able to put out cigarettes on their callused soles.

steps.action: Besides high skills and constant rehearsals, mar
steps.text: Besides high skills and constant rehearsals, marinera also demands great physical effort and sacrifices from the dancers, who must exercise and have a proper diet.

steps.action: Men, on the other hand, have intense practice on
steps.text: Men, on the other hand, have intense practice on "zapateo" and the different steps of the dance until they master them.

steps.action: A marinera norteña dancer leaves aside social li
steps.text: A marinera norteña dancer leaves aside social life, family reunions and resting hours in order to dedicate time to his/hers passion.

steps.action: It's mandatory that the women dance barefoot.
steps.text: It's mandatory that the women dance barefoot.
